english: dhist countries in northern Tibet, esp. Kho-ten; acc. to others in northern India or Nepal.
or li-k'a-ra (li kha ra) Cs. a sort of sugar.
Glr. and elsewh., an orange-coloured powder, acc. to Liś. red lead, minium.
Cs.: 'n. of a province of Tibet near the Chinese frontier', li-t'aṅ-pa (li thang pa) inhabitant of that province.
squinting, squint-eyed Sch., li-ba mig (li ba mig) squinting eyes Sch.
n. of a noble family of ancient India, often mentioned in the history of Buddha Dzl., Gyatch.
v. li (li) III.
Hind. नीलाम, acc. to Shakspeare from the Portuguese leilam, auction, public sale.
1. Ssk. लवङ्ग cloves Med., C. — 2. Hind. इलाएची cardamom W.
S g., Sch.: 'malachite'.
Ssk. 1. sign, mark. — 2. the image of an enemy which is burnt in the sbyin-srég (sbyin sreg) in order thus to kill him by witchcraft Lt. — 3. membrum virile Pth.
a large hornet Sik.

dangling, waving, floating, in the wind Mil.; sprin żig liṅ byuṅ-bas (sprin zhig ling byung bas) a floating cloud? Mil.; *liṅ-liṅ cò-ce* (*ling ling co ce*) W. to dangle, to hang dangling, e.g. on the gallows, *liṅ-liṅ sé-ce* (*ling ling se ce*) W. to swing, to see-saw; rkaṅ-lag pra liṅ-ṅé ₀dug-pa (rkang lag pra ling nge 'dug pa) an infant struggling with hands and feet Pth.
or liṅ-t'óg (ling thog) a film or pellicle on the eye Med.

C., also liṅ-po (ling po) or liṅ (ling) alone, a whole piece, liṅ gčig (ling gcig) of one piece, liṅ bżi (ling bzhi) four pieces or parts, = rnám-pa (rnam pa); gsér-gyi liṅ-ba (gser gyi ling ba) Cs.: a piece of unwrought gold; dar-liṅ (dar ling) Cs. a piece of silk; liṅ-gis ₀dril-ba (ling gis 'dril ba) to pack up into a parcel, to roll up into one packet Sch.
gratings, lattice Cs.
v. liṅ-ṅé (ling nge).

a hunting or chase in which a number of people are engaged; dmág-liṅs (dmag lings) id. (cf. kyi-ra (kyi ra)); byá-liṅs (bya lings) Cs. falconry, hawking; liṅs-la ₀gró-ba (lings la 'gro ba) to go a shooting, a hunting; liṅs ₀débs-pa (lings 'debs pa) Sch. to hunt, to arrange a hunting party; liṅs gtóṅ-ba (lings gtong ba) to get by hunting, to hunt down, liṅs btáṅ-ba (lings btang ba) what has been got by hunting, game shot or caught; liṅs-pa (lings pa) hunter, huntsman, liṅs-pa-mo (lings pa mo) huntress Cs.; liṅs-kyi (lings kyi) hound, liṅs-k'ra (lings khra) hunting falcon or hawk.
hand-mill W. (?)
Sch.: quite round or globular; dkár-por liṅs-te (dkar por lings te) Pth.: prob.: being quite white, cf. *ldiṅs-se* (*ldings se*) Ld. quite.
all, Ld.: *lib du-ċe* (*lib du ce*) to sweep all together with the hands; C.: *k'á-we lib kab soṅ* (*kha we lib kab song*) all being covered with snow.
1. knag, knot, snag, = ₀dzér-pa ('dzer pa); *lu-big* (*lu big*) knot-hole Ts. — 2. num. for 86.
(perh. a misspelling for lugs-koṅ (lugs kong)?) crucible for gold and silver Sch.

diminutive of lug (lug), lamb, frq.; lu-gu-rgyúd (lu gu rgyud) 1. rope to which the lambs are fastened, or strung; hence 2. small chain, e.g. watch-chain, chain or row of stitches on knitting-needles; lace-trimming and the like.
1. vb. to cough, to throw up phlegm, to clear the throat. — 2. sbst. the cough Cs.
Sb. pool containing a spring, ground full of springs, lú-ma-ċan (lu ma can) rich in springs.
the fruit of some thorny shrubs, śib-śi-lu-lu (shib shi lu lu) hip, fruit of the wild rose-tree, tser-stár-lu-lu (tser star lu lu) berry of Hippophaë.

sheep, *dó-lug* (*do lug*), śi-lug (shi lug), bsád-lug* (bsad lug) W. sheep for slaughter. — lúg-k'yu (lug khyu) flock of sheep. — lúg-gu (lug gu) v. lú-gu (lu gu). — lug-sgál (lug sgal) sheep's load — lug-ṅál-ba (lug ngal ba) and lug-čuṅ-ba (lug chung ba) names of medicinal herbs Cs., Wdn. — lug-snyíd (lug snyid) Sch. wether. — lug-t'úg (lug thug) ram B, C.; lug-t'úg-gi rwa dbyibs (lug thug gi rwa dbyibs) like a ram's horn Wdn.; rgya-ru-lug-t'úg (rgya ru lug thug) a Saiga ram S.g. —
